title: Spacetime Curvature
definition: The alteration of spacetime as a result of mass and energy, according to Einstein's theory of general relativity.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Think of spacetime like a rubber sheet stretched out. When heavy objects are placed on it, the space around them bends, just as a weight would cause a dent in the sheet.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: In Einstein's general relativity, gravity is not a force but the curvature of spacetime caused by mass and energy. The presence of mass distorts the fabric of spacetime around it, causing objects to move along curved paths.
difficulty_levels.expert: Einstein's theory of general relativity unifies gravity with the other fundamental forces by describing them as curvature in a four-dimensional space called spacetime. This concept revolutionized our understanding of gravity, leading to predictions such as gravitational waves.

extra_fields.insight: Gravity is not a force but the curvature of spacetime caused by mass and energy
